mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in the Consumer Price Index reflects rising inflation expectations which compress real discount rates and force the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days as investors demand higher nominal compensation.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman rank correlation between CPIAUCSL month-over-month change and 20-day forward yield change is non-positive for any 3-year window during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a perceived natural rate (6.5%), labor market tightness signals future wage pressures and faster policy normalization, causing the 10-year yield to rise within the next 5 trading days as markets front-run Fed action.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses below 6.5%, the 5-day forward yield change is negative in more than 40% of instances over the validation sample, or the average yield change is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong real GDP growth and a federal funds rate below 1% create a joint expectation of imminent monetary tightening, as the output gap closes and the Fed must normalize rates, pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: early expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are simultaneously met, the 20-day forward yield change is negative in more than 50% of episodes, or the interaction term coefficient in a linear regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime (CPI YoY below 2%), a rise in industrial production signals disinflationary supply-side growth that lowers inflation risk premia, causing the 10-year yield to fall over the next 20 trading days, the opposite of the normal pro-growth effect.
failure_condition: During periods with CPIAUCSL YoY below 2%, the 20-day forward yield change is positively correlated with INDPRO month-over-month change (Rank IC > 0) over any 3-year rolling window, or the negative correlation is not significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in housing starts signals robust residential investment and multiplier effects on overall demand, increasing long-term borrowing needs and lifting the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days as the economy transitions to a higher growth path.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between HOUST 3-month change and 60-day forward yield change is consistently non-positive over any 5-year rolling window, or the predictive accuracy (hit rate) fails to exceed 55% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail sales growth exceeds 5% year-over-year, it signals strong consumer demand that is likely to translate into upward pressure on prices and bond yields, with the 10-year Treasury yield rising within the next 5 trading days as markets price in higher growth.
failure_condition: Following RSAFS YoY growth exceeding 5%, the 5-day forward yield change is negative in more than 45% of triggered dates, or the mean yield change is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10) over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: The joint realization of strong payroll gains (above 200k per month) and moderate inflation (CPI YoY below 3%) creates a 'goldilocks' environment where real growth expectations improve without an inflation scare, pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days as the risk premium compresses.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change > 200k and CPIAUCSL YoY < 3%
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, the 20-day forward yield change is negative in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect (PAYEMS * inflation dummy) in a multivariate regression has a negative sign and is not significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime (federal funds rate below 0.25%), a rise in inflation is interpreted as reducing real interest rates without an imminent tightening response, which leads to a decline in nominal 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days as real rates fall and term premiums shrink.
failure_condition: During ZLB periods, the 20-day forward yield change is positively correlated with CPIAUCSL month-over-month change (Rank IC > 0 over any 2-year window), or the negative correlation is not statistically significant at the 10% level.
